name: Port Louis
summary: Port Louis is the capital of Mauritius, a sunny island in the Indian Ocean east of Madagascar. It sits beside a busy harbour with green volcanic mountains poking up behind the city.
funFacts: The dodo lived only on Mauritius and was wiped out by the 1680s after sailors and the dogs, pigs and rats they brought hunted them.
Mauritian Creole mixes French with words from Africa, India and Madagascar, so 'how are you?' becomes 'ki manyer?' instead of a French sentence.
At Chamarel, sand falls naturally into seven different colours — red, brown, violet, green, blue, purple and yellow — made from old volcanic rock.
Mauritius is the only African country where Hinduism is the biggest religion, but Christian, Muslim and Buddhist festivals are also national holidays.
The Blue Penny Museum in Port Louis keeps two of the world's rarest stamps in a dark room and lights them up for just ten minutes each hour.
thingsToDo: Stroll around Le Caudan Waterfront for shops, snacks and boats, just minutes from the cruise terminal.
Sniff vanilla, chillies and tropical fruit at the bustling Central Market near Place d'Armes.
Hike up to the Citadel at Fort Adelaide for breezy views over the harbour and city.
Visit Aapravasi Ghat, a UNESCO site where Indian workers first stepped ashore in the 1800s.
Meet a reconstructed dodo skeleton at the Natural History Museum in the Mauritius Institute.
Take a taxi to Pamplemousses Garden to see giant water lilies and slow-moving giant tortoises.

wildlife.name: Dodo
wildlife.description: Chunky flightless bird that lived only on Mauritius before sailors and rats wiped it out.

wildlife.name: Mauritius Kestrel
wildlife.description: Small brown falcon that nearly vanished but was saved with just four birds left.

wildlife.name: Pink Pigeon
wildlife.description: Soft pink dove found only on Mauritius, also rescued from the edge of extinction.

wildlife.name: Aldabra Giant Tortoise
wildlife.description: Huge slow reptile that can live over 150 years and weigh as much as a fridge.

localWord.meaning: Hello / good morning in Mauritian Creole.

images.alt: Port Louis city and harbour seen from a mountain peak with green hills around it.
